revision.comment: Text replacement - "|式内社" to "|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Shikinaisha of" to "Shikinaisha in"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Takakuramusubimiko-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Hokotori-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Hokotori Shrine

(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Mii-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Mii Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Inari-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Inari Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.3, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 3
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.4, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 4
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) == Visitor Information == ; Location * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 ; Transportation Access * Railway: Take the {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Meijo Line|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|lt=Meijo Line|lt_ja=名城線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and alight at {{ill|Nishitakakura Station|ja|西高蔵駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; it is about a 5‐minute walk. ; Surroundings * {{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Takakuramusubimiko-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Hokotori-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Hokotori Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Shingu-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Shingu Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Mii-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Mii Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Inari-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Inari Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.3, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 3
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.4, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 4

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Takakuramusubimiko-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Hokotori-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Hokotori Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Shingu-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Shingu Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Mii-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Mii Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Inari-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Inari Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.3, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 3
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.4, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 4

{{ill|Meijo Line|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|lt=Meijo Line|lt_ja=名城線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and alight at {{ill|Nishitakakura Station|ja|西高蔵駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; it is about a 5‐minute walk. ; Surroundings * Place Names Series, Volume 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=978-4582490237|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * Other Books ** {{Cite revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Takakuramusubimiko-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Hokotori-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Hokotori Shrine

(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Mii-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Mii Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Inari-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Inari Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.3, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 3
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.4, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 4
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) == Visitor Information == ; Location * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 ; Transportation Access * Railway: Take the {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Meijo Line|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|lt=Meijo Line|lt_ja=名城線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and alight at {{ill|Nishitakakura Station|ja|西高蔵駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; it is about a 5‐minute walk. ; Surroundings * {{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiwa|qq=}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Ward|qq=}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|qq=}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-shita|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|qq=}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|qq=}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|qq=}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Ward|qq=}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|qq=}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-shita|qq=}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokukokushi|qq=}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa era|qq=}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi District|qq=}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|qq=}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genki era|qq=}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genwa era|qq=}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō era|qq=}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|qq=}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa era|qq=}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa era|qq=}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokukokushi|qq=}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa era|qq=}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|qq=}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmandokoro|qq=}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii

{{神社 |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiwa|qq=}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Ward|qq=}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|qq=}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-shita|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|qq=}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|qq=}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|ja|6月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|qq=}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}} {{ill|Atsuta Ward|ja|熱田区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Ward|qq=}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|qq=}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakura-shita|ja|高倉下|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|lt_ja=高倉下命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-shita|qq=}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokukokushi|qq=}}), the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa era|qq=}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|lt=Aichi District|lt_ja=愛智郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi District|qq=}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|qq=}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki era|ja|元亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genki era|qq=}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genwa era|ja|元和 (日本)|lt=Genwa|lt_ja=元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genwa era|qq=}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō era|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō era|qq=}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|qq=}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa era|qq=}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa era|qq=}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rokukokushi|ja|六国史|lt=National Histories|lt_ja=国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokukokushi|qq=}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa era|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa era|qq=}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|qq=}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kaijin Shrine|ja|海神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|ja|大政所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmandokoro|qq=}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii
(July 2013 (Heisei 25)) Takakuramusubimiko-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall

(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Inari-sha Shrine, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Inari Shrine
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.3, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 3
(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) Takakura Kofun No.4, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|Takakura Kofun No. 4
