Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|1=Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580543}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories}}), the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|en|Aichi District|WD=Q552687|lt=Aichi District}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki era}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genwa}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|en|Watatsumi Shrine|WD=Q123530063|lt=Kaijin Shrine}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木俣神|1=Ki no mata no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092833}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|WD=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii

(March 2021 (Reiwa 3)) == Visitor Information == ; Location * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 ; Transportation Access * Railway: Take the {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|en|Nagoya Municipal Subway|WD=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} {{ill|Meijō Line|en|Meijō Line|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijo Line}} and alight at {{ill|Nishi Takakura Station|en|Nishi Takakura Station|WD=Q903624|lt=Nishitakakura Station}}; it is about a 5‐minute walk. ; Surroundings * {{ill|Takakura Site|ja|高蔵遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Site|1=Takakura Site|comment=changed Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|1=Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580543}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories}}), the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|en|Aichi District|WD=Q552687|lt=Aichi District}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki era}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genwa}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|en|Watatsumi Shrine|WD=Q123530063|lt=Kaijin Shrine}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木俣神|1=Ki no mata no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092833}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|WD=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii

Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 ; Transportation Access * Railway: Take the {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|en|Nagoya Municipal Subway|WD=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} {{ill|Meijō Line|en|Meijō Line|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijo Line}} and alight at {{ill|Nishi Takakura Station|en|Nishi Takakura Station|WD=Q903624|lt=Nishitakakura Station}}; it is about a 5‐minute walk. ; Surroundings * {{ill|Takakura Site|ja|高蔵遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Site|1=Takakura Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672782}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine History Document “Guidebook of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine” * On-site Explanatory Board * Publications [[co:commons:Category:Takakuramusubimiko-jinja]][[ja:高座結御子神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Takakuramusubimiko-jinja]][[ja:高座結御子神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takakuramusubimiko-jinja]][[ja:高座結御子神社]] {{Infobox religious building |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|1=Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580543}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories}}), the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|en|Aichi District|WD=Q552687|lt=Aichi District}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki era}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genwa}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|en|Watatsumi Shrine|WD=Q123530063|lt=Kaijin Shrine}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木俣神|1=Ki no mata no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092833}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|WD=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii

Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|1=Takakuraji}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun|1=Danpusan Kofun}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|1=Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580543}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}), the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|en|Aichi District|WD=Q552687|lt=Aichi District|1=Aichi District}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki era|1=Genki (era)}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genwa|1=Genna}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa|1=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|en|Watatsumi Shrine|WD=Q123530063|lt=Kaijin Shrine|1=Watatsumi Shrine (disambiguation)}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木俣神|1=Kimata-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092833}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|WD=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro|1=Ōmandokoro}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, [[co:commons:Category:Takakuramusubimiko-jinja]][[ja:高座結御子神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Takakuramusubimiko-jinja]][[ja:高座結御子神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takakuramusubimiko-jinja]][[ja:高座結御子神社]] {{Infobox religious building |名称 = Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (高座結御子神社) |image = [[File:Takakura-musubi-miko-jinja Shrine Haiden, Takakura-cho Atsuta Ward Nagoya 2021.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|1=Takakuraji}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun|1=Danpusan Kofun}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|1=Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580543}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}), the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|en|Aichi District|WD=Q552687|lt=Aichi District|1=Aichi District}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki era|1=Genki (era)}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genwa|1=Genna}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa|1=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|en|Watatsumi Shrine|WD=Q123530063|lt=Kaijin Shrine|1=Watatsumi Shrine (disambiguation)}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木俣神|1=Kimata-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092833}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|WD=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro|1=Ōmandokoro}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii

(名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 ; Transportation Access * Railway: Take the {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|en|Nagoya Municipal Subway|WD=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} {{ill|Meijō Line|en|Meijō Line|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijo Line|1=Meijō Line}} and alight at {{ill|Nishi Takakura Station|en|Nishi Takakura Station|WD=Q903624|lt=Nishitakakura Station|1=Nishi Takakura Station}}; it is about a 5‐minute walk. ; Surroundings * {{ill|Takakura Site|ja|高蔵遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Site|1=Q11672782|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672782}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine History Document Haiden
(2021 ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} 3) March) |所在地 = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}} 9-9 |ISO = JP-23 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 8 |lat_sec = 7.47 |lon_deg = 136 |lon_min = 54 |lon_sec = 16.03 |祭神 = {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|1=Takakuraji}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|lt=Myoujindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}})
{{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} Keigai-Sessha |創建 = (traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686) |本殿 = {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}} |神事 = }} '''Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine''' (Takakuramusubimiko-jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|ja|高蔵町 (名古屋市)|lt=Takakura-cho|lt_ja=高蔵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura-cho (Nagoya)|1=Takakura-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56449178}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myoujindai|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and a Keigai-Sessha of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. Like Atsuta Shrine, it enshrines the ancestral deity of Owari. Serving as the guardian and tutelary deity of the Takakura area, it is deeply revered locally and affectionately called “Takakura-sama.” == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deity is the following one figureShrine History Document.: * '''{{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakura-shita-no-mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}''' (Takakura-shita-no-mikoto) == History == === Founding === The founding is unknown. According to tradition, it may date to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (673–686), or it is said to have been established around the same time as the main shrine of Atsuta Shrine{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Regarding the Keigai-Sessha of Atsuta Shrine—including Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine—there is a theory correlating its origins with the distribution of various Owari powers during the Kofun period. In this context, it is speculated that the dominant forces on the Atsuta plateau (such as {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun|1=Danpusan Kofun}}, {{ill|Hakuchou Kofun|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Hakuchou Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchou Kofun|1=Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580543}}, and {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}) absorbed other powers, resulting in the incorporation of various shrines into Atsuta Shrine’s divine genealogyShinshu Nagoya City History, Volume 1, Nagoya City, 1997, pp. 486–488.. === General History === According to the national histories ({{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}), the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (836) contains a passage listing three deities of Atsuta Shrine—the Hiwari-mikogami, Mago-waka-mikogami, and the deity of Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Atsuta Ōkami Mikogami) – among the renowned deities{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. In the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5th year (927) version of the ''Engishiki'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}), in Owari Province’s {{ill|Aichi District|en|Aichi District|WD=Q552687|lt=Aichi District|1=Aichi District}}, it is recorded as “Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine Myoujindai” and is listed alongside the {{ill|Myoujindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoujindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. Moreover, in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}, it is recorded either as “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. During the construction in the {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki era|1=Genki (era)}} 2nd year (1571), there was a donation from {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}; in the Edo period, in the {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genwa|1=Genna}} 4th year (1618), repairs were carried out by {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa|1=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, and in the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} 3rd year (1686), when Atsuta Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar repairs were made to this shrine{{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. The shrine building, originally in the {{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|1=Owari-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465322}} style with a vermilion finish, was almost entirely destroyed by wartime damage in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 20th year (1945) and was rebuilt in a form approximating its pre-war appearance in the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 38th year (1963){{Sfn|Takakurayui Mikoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1989}}. === Divine Rank === * Record of the conferral of divine Rank during the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} period: ** On December 12, in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (836), it was listed among the renowned deities (as recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) – noted as “Takakurayui Mikoshi.” * After the era of the National Histories: ** Either Senior Second Rank[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} ''Shinja Kakoroku, Volume 1'' (Imperial Studies Institute, 1902, National Diet Library Digital Collection) 413 lines], or Upper Senior Second Rank{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf ''Domestic Shinmyōchō'']]} (Bunsei 13 [1830] transcribed combined edition, Aichi Prefectural Library “Precious Wa Books Digital Library”) 6 lines., or Junior Second Rank{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}} (in the {{ill|Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|ja|尾張国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kokunai Shinmyōchō|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11041830}}) – the inscription being “Takakura Myōjin” or “Takakura Myōjin”{{Sfn|Miya-ki|2012|p=28}}. Note that the Ranking varies among manuscripts. == Subordinate Shrines == Within the precincts, the following four subsidiary shrines are enshrined. In the early modern period, it is said that there were also the {{ill|Mitarai Shrine|ja|御手洗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|en|Watatsumi Shrine|WD=Q123530063|lt=Kaijin Shrine|1=Watatsumi Shrine (disambiguation)}}. * {{ill|Hokotori Shrine|ja|鉾取社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Hokotori-no-Kami|ja|鉾取神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokotori-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shingu Shrine|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Mii Shrine|ja|御井社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Mii-no-Kami|ja|御井神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木俣神|1=Kimata-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092833}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} *: It is said that the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} (later known as {{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|WD=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro|1=Ōmandokoro}}) once took the young Hideyoshi with her to pray for his rise in life, which is why this shrine is also known by the alternate name “Taikō Shusse Inari.” == Festivals == * Inari Shrine Festival (on Hatsu-uma day) * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Spring Grand Festival (old Hatsu-uma day) * Child Custody Festival (April 3) *: A custom in which young children are entrusted to the enshrined deity until the age of 15, praying for healthy growth and protection from pests, with a subsequent thanksgiving visit once safely grown. * Annual Festival (June 1) * Mii Festival (on Doyō-iri day) *: It is believed that if a child peers into the well of Mii Shrine on this day, it brings benefits of pest protection. * Takakura Inari Kōsha, Autumn Grand Festival (November 8) == Gallery == Takakuramusubimiko-jinja torii.JPG|South Torii
